GREEN.label: GREEN
GREEN.conditions: No CRITICAL, no HIGH, 3 or fewer MEDIUM findings
GREEN.action: Document compliance posture; schedule annual review

YELLOW.label: YELLOW
YELLOW.conditions: 1-2 HIGH findings, OR 4 or more MEDIUM findings (no CRITICAL)
YELLOW.action: Address HIGH findings; review with DPO

RED.label: RED
RED.conditions: 1 or more CRITICAL findings, OR 3 or more HIGH findings
RED.action: Stop processing PII until CRITICAL issues resolved; immediate DPO/lawyer consultation

CRITICAL.description: Immediate violation risk — requires priority action
examples: Passwords stored in plain text
PII (PESEL, SSN, credit card) logged to console or log files
Personal data transmitted over unencrypted connection (HTTP, sslmode=disable)
Hardcoded database credentials in source code
IBAN or credit card numbers in application code
No deletion mechanism (Art. 17 violation)
Criminal data (Art. 10) without documented authorization
Special category data (Art. 9) without documented legal basis
SSL/TLS certificate validation disabled
Analytics SDKs initialized without consent check
Logging locale-specific IDs (PESEL, Personalausweis, NIE, INSEE, Codice Fiscale)
No consent management library or consent mechanism detected
No privacy policy found in codebase
No data deletion endpoint (Art. 17)
Pre-ticked consent checkboxes
Bundled consent for multiple purposes
CORS wildcard with credentials
CSRF protection disabled
Retention set to unlimited/forever
PII in email subject lines
Tracking cookies set without consent (ePrivacy violation)
Automated decision-making without human review option
Art. 8 children data without age verification
Art. 22 credit scoring without explainability
Request body logged (may contain PII depending on endpoint)
Soft-delete without confirmed hard-delete mechanism
IP addresses in code without purpose documentation
PII variable names without data minimization documentation
Tracking pixel without consent verification (ePrivacy consideration)
PII in push notifications
No data export endpoint (Art. 20)
Missing processing purpose comments in code
No privacy-by-design review in new modules
Consent patterns found in i18n/locale files only
Test file matches downgraded from higher severity
Consent management library detected
Data deletion endpoint found
Privacy policy reference found
Data portability endpoint found

art8_children.title: Children's Data
art8_children.description: Processing data of minors requires specific consent mechanisms and age verification. Member states set age between 13-16.

art9_special_categories.title: Special Category Data
art9_special_categories.description: Health, biometric, genetic, racial/ethnic, political, religious, trade union, sexual orientation, philosophical beliefs, sex life data. Requires explicit consent or other Art. 9(2) exception.

art10_criminal.title: Criminal Data
art10_criminal.description: Processing criminal convictions/offences requires official authority or specific national law. Stricter than Art. 9.

art22_automated.title: Automated Decision-Making
art22_automated.description: Automated decisions with significant effects on individuals require transparency, human review option, and right to contest.

art26_joint_controllers.title: Joint Controllers
art26_joint_controllers.description: Joint processing arrangements require transparent liability allocation, documented agreements, and clear data subject communication.

art27_eu_representatives.title: Representatives in EU
art27_eu_representatives.description: Non-EU data controllers must appoint an EU representative; required if subjects in EU, GDPR applies, but controller not established in EU.

art35_dpia.title: DPIA Required
art35_dpia.description: High-risk processing requires a Data Protection Impact Assessment before commencing.

art37_dpo_required.title: DPO Required
art37_dpo_required.description: Public authorities and organizations whose core activities require systematic monitoring must appoint a Data Protection Officer.

art44_transfers.title: International Data Transfers
art44_transfers.description: Transfers outside EEA require adequacy decision, SCCs, BCRs, or other Art. 46 mechanism.

tier1.title: Tier 1 (Articles 8, 11, 25-39, 41(4), 42-43)
tier1.description: Less severe violations — data minimization, security, design, organizational measures

tier2.title: Tier 2 (All other GDPR articles)
tier2.description: All remaining GDPR violations — consent, rights, transfers, DPA requirements

scoring_algorithm.description: Evaluate in order: RED first, then YELLOW, then GREEN. First match wins.

finding_categories.pii_pattern: Personal data directly in code — regex, variable names, hardcoded values
finding_categories.pii_locale: Country-specific personal identifiers (PESEL, NIE, INSEE, etc.)
finding_categories.risky_practice: Dangerous technical practices — logging, credentials, encryption
finding_categories.consent_violation: Violations of consent principles — pre-checked, bundled, no withdrawal
finding_categories.special_category_art9: Health, biometric, genetic, racial, political, religious, union, sexual orientation, philosophical beliefs, sex life data
finding_categories.data_retention: Storage limitation violations — unlimited retention, soft-delete without expiry
finding_categories.third_party_transfer: Personal data sent to third-party services
finding_categories.tracking_without_consent: Tracking cookies and pixels without prior consent (ePrivacy)
finding_categories.pii_in_notification: Personal data in notification subjects or bodies
